name: English
comment: "Chaotic spelling with nearly no grammar rules."

name: Norwegian
comment: "Very simple grammar with relatively consistent pronunciation."

name: Danish
comment: "Simple grammar but notorious for 'swallowed' sounds."

name: Swedish
comment: "Musical pitch accent and straightforward grammar."

name: Dutch
comment: "Predictable spelling but tricky guttural sounds."

name: Spanish
regex: "[A-Za-z帽谩茅铆贸煤眉脩脕脡脥脫脷脺]"
comment: "Gold standard for phonetic consistency; mid-level verb complexity."

name: Italian
regex: "[A-Za-z脿猫茅矛貌贸霉脌脠脡脤脪脫脵]"
comment: "Standard version is perfectly phonetic with logical grammar."

name: Bulgarian
comment: "Highly phonetic Cyrillic; rare Slavic language without cases."

name: Macedonian
comment: "Very similar to Bulgarian; highly transparent reading."

name: Portuguese
regex: "[A-Za-z脿谩芒茫莽茅锚铆贸么玫煤脌脕脗脙脟脡脢脥脫脭脮脷]"
comment: "Consistent standard phonetics but heavy on verb tenses."

name: Romanian
comment: "Phonetic Latin language that strangely preserved cases."

name: Catalan
regex: "[A-Za-z脿猫茅铆茂貌贸煤眉莽脌脠脡脥脧脪脫脷脺脟]"
comment: "Bridge between Spanish and French; mostly consistent."

name: Welsh
regex: "[A-Za-z芒锚卯么没诺欧脗脢脦脭脹糯哦]"
comment: "Logical spelling once the unique Celtic system is learned."

name: French
regex: "[A-Za-z脿芒忙莽茅猫锚毛卯茂么艙霉没眉每脌脗脝脟脡脠脢脣脦脧脭艗脵脹脺鸥]"
comment: "Predictable reading but extremely redundant silent letters."

name: Serbo-Croatian
comment: "Strictly phonetic ('write as you speak') but complex cases."

name: Latin
comment: "Highly consistent classic pronunciation with rigid declensions."

name: Maltese
comment: "Semitic roots with a Latin-influenced grammatical structure."

name: Albanian
comment: "Independent branch; consistent spelling but isolated grammar."

name: Latvian
regex: "[A-Za-z膩膷膿模墨姆募艈拧奴啪膧膶膾蘑莫亩幕艆艩弄沤]"
comment: "Complex Baltic grammar with consistent vowel markings."

name: German
comment: "Very consistent standard rules but rigid syntax and cases."

name: Greek
regex: "[伪-蠅螒-惟维苇萎委蠈蠉蠋螁螆螇螉螌螏螐]"
comment: "Consistent reading but difficult historical spelling."

name: Irish
comment: "Archaic spelling meets complex initial mutations."

name: Finnish
comment: "Perfect phonetics but 15 cases of grammatical torture."

name: Estonian
comment: "Similar to Finnish; transparent but case-heavy."

name: Basque
comment: "Pure isolate; easy to read

name: Czech
regex: "[A-Za-z谩膷膹茅臎铆艌贸艡拧钮煤暖媒啪脕膶膸脡臍脥艊脫艠艩扭脷女脻沤]"
comment: "Strictly phonetic but one of the hardest Slavic grammars."

name: Slovak
regex: "[A-Za-z谩盲膷膹茅铆暮木艌贸么艜拧钮煤媒啪脕脛膶膸脡脥墓慕艊脫脭艛艩扭脷脻沤]"
comment: "Phonetic and regular; slightly more 'central' than Czech."

name: Polish
regex: "[A-Za-z膮膰臋艂艅贸艣藕偶膭膯臉艁艃脫艢殴呕]"
comment: "Consistent rules but dense clusters and 7 cases."

name: Icelandic
regex: "[A-Za-z谩茅铆贸煤媒镁忙枚脕脡脥脫脷脻脼脝脰]"
comment: "Preserved Viking grammar with strict standard phonetics."

name: Lithuanian
regex: "[A-Za-z膮膷臋臈寞拧懦奴啪膭膶臉臇漠艩挪弄沤]"
comment: "Archaic Indo-European features with extreme complexity."

name: Slovene
comment: "Consistent spelling; unique for keeping the dual number."

name: Ukrainian
regex: "[邪-褟袗-携褨褩褦覒袉袊袆覑]"
comment: "Highly melodic and more phonetic than Russian."

name: Belarusian
comment: "Complex Slavic grammar with very phonetic spelling."

name: Hungarian
regex: "[A-Za-z谩茅铆贸枚艖煤眉疟脕脡脥脫脰艕脷脺虐]"
comment: "Agglutinative nightmare with logic unrelated to other EU languages."

name: Russian
comment: "Heavy grammar and vowel reduction even in the standard."
